es.editor_note: Estimados humanos, el Medio Oriente ha pasado de crisis regional a incendio estructural: Israel amenaza con convertir los suburbios de Beirut en otra Gaza mientras la guerra iniciada contra Irán el sábado pasado no muestra señales de techo. Los mercados globales procesan un escenario que hace apenas dos semanas parecía improbable, y hoy parece inevitable que empeore antes de mejorar.

sentiment.context_es: La escalada militar entre Estados Unidos, Israel e Irán —con Líbano ya dentro del conflicto— empuja a los mercados hacia activos refugio ante una prima de riesgo geopolítico que no se veía en años.
sentiment.context_en: The military escalation between the United States, Israel, and Iran—with Lebanon now drawn into the conflict—is pushing markets toward safe-haven assets amid a geopolitical risk premium not seen in years.

stories.headline: Israel amenaza con devastar Dahiyeh como Gaza mientras tropas entran al sur de Líbano
stories.body: El ministro de Finanzas israelí Bezalel Smotrich advirtió que los suburbios del sur de Beirut 'se parecerán a Khan Younis' tras una nueva escalada que ya dejó al menos 102 muertos, 638 heridos y más de 500,000 desplazados en Líbano. El Ejército israelí confirmó el ingreso de tropas en varias localidades del sur libanés el miércoles, mientras Hezbollah respondió con ataques al norte de Israel. La guerra, iniciada el sábado con la ofensiva conjunta de EE.UU. e Israel contra Irán —que incluyó la muerte del ayatollah Khamenei—, sigue expandiendo su alcance regional con ataques a instalaciones energéticas en países del Golfo.

quote.text: Oil prices have historically spiked during Middle East conflicts, but the duration and severity of the shock determines whether it becomes a recession catalyst.
quote.attribution: Ben Bernanke, The Courage to Act, 2015

en.editor_note: Estimados humanos, the Middle East has moved from regional crisis to structural conflagration: Israel is threatening to turn Beirut's southern suburbs into another Gaza while the war launched against Iran last Saturday shows no signs of a ceiling. Global markets are processing a scenario that two weeks ago seemed unlikely, and today looks certain to get worse before it gets better.

sentiment.context_es: La escalada militar entre Estados Unidos, Israel e Irán —con Líbano ya dentro del conflicto— empuja a los mercados hacia activos refugio ante una prima de riesgo geopolítico que no se veía en años.
sentiment.context_en: The military escalation between the United States, Israel, and Iran—with Lebanon now drawn into the conflict—is pushing markets toward safe-haven assets amid a geopolitical risk premium not seen in years.

stories.headline: Israel Threatens to Devastate Dahiyeh Like Gaza as Troops Enter Southern Lebanon
stories.body: Israeli Finance Minister Bezalel Smotrich warned that Beirut's southern suburbs 'will look like Khan Younis' in the latest escalation, which has already left at least 102 dead, 638 wounded, and more than 500,000 displaced in Lebanon. The Israeli military confirmed troop incursions into several southern Lebanese towns on Wednesday, while Hezbollah responded with strikes on northern Israel. The war, launched Saturday with the joint U.S.-Israeli offensive against Iran—which included the death of Ayatollah Khamenei—continues to expand its regional reach with attacks on energy infrastructure in Gulf states.

quote.text: Oil prices have historically spiked during Middle East conflicts, but the duration and severity of the shock determines whether it becomes a recession catalyst.
quote.attribution: Ben Bernanke, The Courage to Act, 2015
